fieldOpsPage.title: Batch import, pre-survey planning, and camera intake
fieldOpsPage.body: Use this page for SD-card batch scan preview, pre-survey species recommendations, infrared camera upload, and multi-group species entry.

batchResults.title: Scan results
batchResults.noFiles: No files found in the specified directory.

preSurveyResults.title: Pre-survey species list
preSurveyResults.noData: No species data available for this region.

appShell.offlineBanner: You are offline. Cached pages remain available, but uploads and live monitoring may pause.
appShell.installMobileBody: Add this app to your home screen for faster field access.
appShell.footerLine1: Species-first acoustic evidence instead of acoustic-index storytelling.
appShell.footerLine2: Desktop for deep review, mobile for field operations and quick decisions.
appShell.backendUnavailableDetail: The frontend cannot reach the API right now.

dashboardPage.title: Species-first acoustic research workbench for evidence, review, and biodiversity interpretation
dashboardPage.body: This platform borrows the short-path desktop usability of BirdNET-Analyzer, but its scientific center is different: species-level evidence, human review, and defensible downstream biodiversity analysis instead of acoustic-index storytelling.
dashboardPage.workflowBody: A short operational loop inspired by BirdNET, extended for review and ecological evidence.
dashboardPage.painPoints: Sugai et al. (2026): pain points to avoid
dashboardPage.evidencePipeline: Evidence pipeline instead of index pipeline
dashboardPage.baselineBody: BirdNET remains useful as a baseline reference. The goal is not to copy its scientific framing, but to learn from its practical workflow while making review and ecological interpretation explicit.
dashboardPage.selectAudio: Select audio file for baseline comparison
dashboardPage.compareFailed: Failed to compare against BirdNET baseline.
dashboardPage.coverageGapBody: {{missing}} species are present in the database but not in the active checkpoint. Current coverage is {{ratio}}.

analyze.title: Analyze audio
analyze.desc: Start with fast, BirdNET-like single-file or batch analysis.

verify.title: Review evidence
verify.desc: Confirm or reject detections before they become ecological evidence.

monitor.title: Monitor sites
monitor.desc: Track live sessions, devices, and accumulating detections over time.

species.title: Interpret biodiversity
species.desc: Use species-level records for diversity, occupancy, and site comparison.

principles.borrowBody: Desktop-first workflow, short path to analysis, batch handling, and practical result inspection.
principles.beyondBody: This platform is designed as a research workbench, not only a recognizer. Review, traceability, and ecological interpretation are first-class features.
principles.speciesBody: Following Sugai et al. (2026), the product centers species-level detections instead of acoustic indices as the basis for biodiversity inference.

painPointItems: Acoustic indices do not identify which species are present.
Index values are difficult to explain in conservation and management decisions.
Statistical validity is inconsistent across taxa, habitats, and recording conditions.
Human-reviewed detections are more defensible than opaque summary scores.
evidenceSteps: CNN and optional BirdNET baseline generate candidate species.
Detections are stored with confidence, timing, and session context.
Humans review uncertain records before ecological interpretation.
Species-level evidence feeds diversity summaries and downstream exports.

verifyPage.badge: Machine output is draft evidence until reviewed
verifyPage.title: Evidence review desk
verifyPage.body: This queue is no longer just a list. You can now filter, inspect context, leave reviewer notes, and approve or reject detections in batches before they flow into interpretation.
verifyPage.sharedNotePlaceholder: Why you confirm or reject this group of records
verifyPage.selectedHint: Batch actions apply your reviewer name and shared note to all selected detections.
verifyPage.noMatches: No detections match the current filters.
verifyPage.noMatchesHint: Try broadening the query or refreshing the queue.
verifyPage.inspectorEmptyBody: Pick a detection from the queue to inspect site history, session composition, and occupancy context.
verifyPage.inspectorBody: Use the context below to decide whether this record is suitable as evidence.

analyzePage.title: Turn each recording into a reviewable evidence package
analyzePage.body: This page stays close to the BirdNET-style analysis experience, but the output is framed for biodiversity work: detected species, confidence, diversity summaries, and assets that can be checked again during human review.
analyzePage.fileTooLarge: Audio file is larger than 100 MB. Please choose a shorter clip.
analyzePage.dropTitle: Drop an audio file here or click to browse
analyzePage.dropHint: Supported formats: WAV, MP3, FLAC, OGG
analyzePage.batchTitle: Batch triage for folders and field campaigns
analyzePage.batchBody: Use this to scan multiple clips quickly before moving the most informative recordings into formal review.
analyzePage.topSignalEmpty: No species exceeded the current threshold.
analyzePage.nextStepReady: Move this recording into Review to confirm uncertain detections before drawing ecological conclusions.
analyzePage.nextStepEmpty: Lower the threshold slightly or inspect the spectrogram before discarding the clip.

diversity.richnessDesc: Number of distinct species observed in this clip.
diversity.shannonDesc: Balances richness with relative evenness across detections.
diversity.simpsonDesc: Higher values indicate detections are not dominated by one species.
diversity.pielouDesc: How evenly detections are distributed across observed species.
diversity.chao1Desc: Estimated richness including likely unseen species.
diversity.totalDesc: Evidence events contributing to the summary above.

analyzePage.spectrogramCompareDesc: Upload two audio files to compare their frequency patterns side by side. Useful for species identification, dialect comparison, and detection verification.
analyzePage.imageAnalysisDesc: Upload a bird photo to extract GPS location, timestamp, and run visual classification. Results are stored for cross-referencing with acoustic detections.
analyzePage.selectImage: Select or drop a bird photo

devicesPage.title: Keep monitoring hardware organized before the evidence starts flowing
devicesPage.body: This page is the operational layer for your low-power field setup: what devices exist, where they are, and whether they are currently contributing to the platform.
devicesPage.syncState: Registry is synchronized with the current backend state.

notes.whyBody: Field monitoring becomes much easier to trust when hardware identity, location, and session provenance stay aligned.
notes.focusBody: This setup favors reliable collection on low-power devices instead of heavier compute workloads.
notes.evidenceBody: Each active device can now feed detections that persist into review rather than disappearing after a session ends.

devicesPage.formBody: Add the device once, then use the generated registry to keep session history and site context traceable.
devicesPage.deviceMapBody: Mapped points help keep deployments, sites, and later detections tied together.
devicesPage.noDevicesBody: Use the form above to add the first field unit.

monitorPage.title: Track what devices are hearing and keep the evidence stream visible
monitorPage.body: Monitoring is not only about staying online. It is about knowing which sessions are active, which species are being detected, and whether those detections are ready to move into review and downstream interpretation.
monitorPage.monitoringModeBody: The platform is currently in {{mode}} mode.
monitorPage.syncState: Session list and dashboard summary are in sync.
monitorPage.sessionsBody: Sessions shown here are the live evidence streams currently feeding the platform.
monitorPage.noSessions: No monitoring sessions are active right now.
monitorPage.noSessionsBody: Connect a device and start streaming to populate this panel.
monitorPage.topSpeciesBody: These counts summarize what is being observed across active sessions right now.
monitorPage.noTopSpeciesBody: Species counts appear here once detections arrive from a running session.

reasons.continuityBody: Realtime detections are now persisted, so monitoring output can flow into review and later analysis instead of disappearing with the session.
reasons.powerBody: The focus stays on stable collection and traceable detections rather than heavy training jobs that your current hardware cannot sustain.
reasons.speciesBody: Counts here are intentionally species-based, keeping the platform aligned with biodiversity interpretation instead of abstract acoustic indices.

monitorPage.surveySitesDesc: Manage field survey sites for systematic acoustic monitoring across regions.

speciesPage.title: Browse species & listen to their songs
speciesPage.body: Click a species to expand and listen to recordings from Xeno-Canto. Songs are categorized by type and region.
speciesPage.searchPlaceholder: Search by Chinese, English, or scientific name
speciesPage.noRecordings: No recordings found. Configure XC API key in Settings.

embeddingsPage.noEmbeddingsForSession: No embeddings available for this session.
embeddingsPage.title: Explore how recordings organize in embedding space after detection
embeddingsPage.body: This page is for the exploratory side of the pipeline: how similar detections cluster, whether species occupy distinct regions in feature space, and which sounds might deserve extra scrutiny as novel or poorly explained events.
embeddingsPage.sessionHint: Use a session from monitoring or analysis history to inspect latent structure.
embeddingsPage.noteWhyBody: They provide a continuous representation of sound structure instead of collapsing everything into one score.
embeddingsPage.noteWatchBody: Tight species clusters suggest learned separation, while overlap can indicate ambiguous calls or sparse training coverage.
embeddingsPage.noteNoveltyBody: Outlying sounds can be useful leads, but they still need review before any ecological claim is made.
embeddingsPage.syncCurrent: Embedding stats and recent sessions are current.
embeddingsPage.loadSessionBody: Choose an available monitoring session or type an id manually to inspect its embedding structure.
embeddingsPage.embeddingMapBody: Two-dimensional view of session embeddings, colored by predicted species.
embeddingsPage.emptyMapBody: Load a session above to see whether detections form interpretable acoustic structure.
embeddingsPage.novelBody: Events far from known cluster centroids can be useful leads, but they are not evidence on their own.
embeddingsPage.novelEmpty: No novelty candidates are currently available for this session.
embeddingsPage.guideTightBody: Usually suggest the model is representing a species consistently across multiple detections.
embeddingsPage.guideMixedBody: May indicate similar vocal structure, noisy detections, or limits in current coverage.
embeddingsPage.guideOutliersBody: Treat as review candidates. They can reflect rare calls, noise, or genuinely interesting unknown events.
embeddingsPage.similarityBody: A session-level view of which species occupy nearby acoustic space.
embeddingsPage.emptySimilarityBody: Once a session has enough embeddings, species-level similarity will appear here.

xenoCantoPage.title: Search Xeno-canto when you need external recordings for context
xenoCantoPage.body: This is a support tool rather than the platform's core evidence path. Use it to gather comparison recordings, inspect call variation, or build reference context around species already under review.
xenoCantoPage.keyHelp: Xeno-canto API v3 now requires an API key. Register and verify your account before saving the key here.
xenoCantoPage.queryPlaceholder: Enter a scientific name, e.g. Gorsachius magnificus
xenoCantoPage.noResults: No matching recordings were found. Double-check the scientific name and try again.

sdmPage.demoBannerBody: The heatmap, detection points, and priority sites shown below are illustrative examples based on literature and expert knowledge — not outputs from a connected SDM pipeline. GBIF and iNaturalist occurrences are fetched live when available.
sdmPage.title: Use habitat suitability as a deployment guide, not a substitute for evidence
sdmPage.body: This page frames SDM as a conservation planning aid. It helps identify where to listen, but the platform's scientific logic still returns to species-level detections, review, and occupancy-aware interpretation.
sdmPage.noteContributesBody: It narrows the search space for monitoring by highlighting likely habitat and climate refugia.
sdmPage.noteCannotReplaceBody: Predicted suitability is not evidence of current presence. Acoustic confirmation remains essential.
sdmPage.noteWhyMattersBody: For a power-limited monitoring system, better site prioritization is often more valuable than more model complexity.
sdmPage.integPrioritizeDesc: Use predicted suitable habitat to place low-power recorders where detection payoff is most likely to be highest.
sdmPage.integValidateDesc: Use species-level detections to confirm whether predicted habitat is actually occupied or simply suitable on paper.
sdmPage.integOccupancyDesc: Repeated acoustic visits help separate non-detection from true absence, which is critical when evaluating SDM outputs.

settingsPage.heroTitle: Inspect what is enabled and keep external access configured cleanly
settingsPage.heroBody: This page is mainly for runtime visibility and key management. It helps confirm which inference features are available without drifting into heavyweight training operations.
settingsPage.noteLightweightBody: The current hardware path favors stable inference and monitoring, not power-hungry retraining jobs.
settingsPage.noteExternalBody: Xeno-canto access is optional, but useful when building reference libraries and checking recordings.
settingsPage.noteScientificBody: Feature status is shown transparently so the platform's conclusions can be interpreted in context.
settingsPage.xcKeyPlaceholder: Paste a new Xeno-canto API key
settingsPage.ebirdKeyNotConfigured: eBird API key is not configured.
settingsPage.ebirdKeyObtainSuffix: . Enables regional species data, hotspot info, and recent observations.
settingsPage.featureSpeciesDesc: Primary species-first workflow for clips and field recordings.
settingsPage.featureBirdnetDesc: Reference engine for comparison, not the platform's main scientific narrative.
settingsPage.featureOodDesc: Helps surface potentially unusual signals for careful review.
settingsPage.featureDeviceDesc: Supports field hardware registration and monitoring sessions.
settingsPage.featureRealtimeDesc: WebSocket-driven monitoring for continuous, low-power deployments.
settingsPage.featureDiversityDesc: Species-based alpha and related summaries built from detections.
settingsPage.featureReportDesc: Exports analysis output into a portable review-friendly report.
settingsPage.featureDarwinCoreDesc: Export detection data in Darwin Core standard format for biodiversity databases.
settingsPage.featureUserAccountsDesc: Multi-user login and role-based access control.
settingsPage.saveEbirdKeyFailed: Failed to save eBird API key.

aboutPage.heroTitle: Learn from BirdNET-Analyzer's product discipline, but follow Sugai et al. (2026) for scientific direction
aboutPage.heroBody: The strategic idea is simple: borrow what BirdNET gets right about desktop usability, then build a stronger ecological workflow around reviewable species evidence. This keeps the product familiar to practitioners while aligning it with the paper's critique of acoustic-index-first reasoning.
aboutPage.panelPlatformTitle: Where This Platform Must Be Different
birdnetLessons: Keep the path from file selection to result inspection extremely short.
Support batch-oriented workflows instead of only one-off demos.
Present detections with timing, confidence, and practical export options.
Feel like a dependable desktop tool, not only a wrapped web page.
platformDifferences: This product treats human review as part of the scientific workflow, not a side feature.
The center of interpretation is species-level evidence rather than abstract acoustic indices.
Monitoring, traceability, and biodiversity summaries are presented as downstream uses of verified detections.
BirdNET is positioned as a baseline and inspiration, not as the conceptual template for ecological inference.
aboutPage.paperSectionTitle: Methods in Ecology and Evolution (Sugai et al., 2026)
aboutPage.paperBoldTitle: Acoustic indices are not useful for biodiversity research
aboutPage.paperBody: is the paper that gives this product its scientific posture. The tool should not merely report sound statistics; it should help researchers build understandable species-level evidence.
aboutPage.painPointsTitle: Pain Points the Product Should Surface

sugaiPainPoints.title: Acoustic indices do not tell you which species are present.
sugaiPainPoints.detail: That makes them weak foundations for conservation action, occupancy analysis, invasive-species detection, and site-level interpretation.

sugaiPainPoints.title: Index behaviour is unstable across habitats and recording conditions.
sugaiPainPoints.detail: A product based only on aggregate sound metrics can look scientific while remaining hard to defend in practice.

sugaiPainPoints.title: Statistical convenience is not ecological evidence.
sugaiPainPoints.detail: The paper argues for more defensible, understandable workflows that connect results to identifiable species.

sugaiPainPoints.title: Protection decisions need interpretable records.
sugaiPainPoints.detail: When a rare species is claimed, researchers need timestamps, clips, context, and review status, not only a changing index value.

aboutPage.productResponsesTitle: Product Responses to Those Pain Points
platformResponses: Run species-level detection on audio segments rather than deriving a biodiversity story from acoustic indices alone.
Store evidence with timing, confidence, session, and site context.
Keep a review queue so people can confirm, reject, or mark uncertain detections.
Use verified records as the basis for diversity summaries, occupancy preparation, exports, and reporting.
aboutPage.flowAnalyzeBody: Fast species detection on files and batches, with practical desktop ergonomics.
aboutPage.flowReviewBody: Move machine output through an explicit human validation step.
aboutPage.flowMonitorBody: Follow devices, sessions, and accumulating evidence over time.
aboutPage.flowInterpretBody: Use validated species records for biodiversity and site-level reasoning.
aboutPage.designRuleBody: When choosing what to build next, prefer features that make detections easier to inspect, verify, trace, compare, and export. De-prioritize features that only add decorative metrics without strengthening ecological interpretability.
